title: On-Demand Theme CSS Loading (40 Themes Loaded Eagerly)

descriptionHistory.description: Convert 40+ eagerly-loaded theme CSS files to on-demand dynamic loading

planSpec.content:
I'll analyze the codebase first to understand the existing structure, then generate the specification.

}

  "file_path": "C:\\Users\\DylanSmart\\Downloads\\dmaker-pr\\apps\\ui\\src\\components\\views\\project-settings-view\\project-theme-section.tsx"

I now have complete understanding. Here is the specification:

---

## 1. Problem Statement

All 40 theme CSS files (~140-200KB total) are loaded synchronously at app startup via `theme-imports.ts`, regardless of which single theme the user has active. This wastes bandwidth and slows initial page load by forcing the browser to parse CSS that will never be used. Users only ever use one theme at a time.

## 2. User Story

As a **user of DMaker**, I want **only my active theme CSS loaded at startup**, so that **the app loads faster with a smaller initial CSS payload**.

## 3. Acceptance Criteria

- **Happy Path - Initial Load**: GIVEN a user with "dracula" as their stored theme, WHEN the app loads, THEN only `dark.css` (base) and `dracula.css` are fetched/loaded; the other 38 theme CSS files are not loaded.
- **Happy Path - Theme Switch**: GIVEN a user on "dracula" theme, WHEN they click "Nord" in settings, THEN `nord.css` is dynamically loaded and the theme switches without flash.
- **Happy Path - Cached Theme**: GIVEN a user who previously loaded "nord" then switched back to "dracula", WHEN they switch to "nord" again, THEN the theme applies instantly from cache without a network request.
- **Happy Path - Hover Preview**: GIVEN a user hovering over theme buttons in the setup wizard (theme-step), WHEN they hover over "Monokai", THEN `monokai.css` is preloaded and the preview applies.
- **Edge Case - System Theme**: GIVEN a user with "system" theme mode, WHEN the app loads, THEN the appropriate base theme (`dark.css` or `light.css`) is loaded based on `prefers-color-scheme`.
- **Edge Case - Light Base Theme**: GIVEN a user with the "light" theme, WHEN the app loads, THEN `light.css` is loaded (it has minimal content, just scrollbar overrides) and no other themes are fetched.
- **Edge Case - Project Theme Override**: GIVEN a user whose project has a custom theme "catppuccin", WHEN the app loads that project, THEN `catppuccin.css` is dynamically loaded.
- **Error Handling - Missing CSS**: GIVEN a corrupted theme name in localStorage, WHEN the app tries to load it, THEN it falls back to "dark" gracefully without crashing.
- **Pre-hydration**: GIVEN any stored theme, WHEN the page loads before React hydration, THEN `applyStoredTheme()` still prevents theme flash (CSS classes applied synchronously; the CSS file is loaded eagerly for the stored theme).

## 4. Technical Context

| Aspect | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Affected Files | `apps/ui/src/lib/theme-loader.ts` (new), `apps/ui/src/styles/theme-imports.ts`, `apps/ui/src/routes/__root.tsx`, `apps/ui/src/app.tsx`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/setup-view/steps/theme-step.tsx`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/settings-view/appearance/appearance-section.tsx`, `apps/ui/src/components/views/project-settings-view/project-theme-section.tsx` |
| Dependencies | None (uses Vite's built-in dynamic `import()` for CSS) |
| Constraints | `applyStoredTheme()` runs synchronously before React hydration - it can apply CSS classes but can't `await`. The CSS must be loaded before or shortly after hydration to avoid flash. Base themes (`dark.css`, `light.css`) should remain synchronous. |
| Patterns to Follow | Existing dynamic import pattern used for xterm modules in `terminal-panel.tsx`. Theme CSS files use class-scoped selectors (`.dracula { ... }`, `:root.dark { ... }`) so they can coexist when loaded. |

## 5. Non-Goals

- Removing or refactoring the theme CSS files themselves
- Changing how `themeOptions` or `ThemeMode` types work
- Adding theme preloading for the settings appearance section or project theme section (only the setup wizard has hover preview; the settings views use click-to-select)
- Code-splitting the theme configuration (`theme-options.ts`) - only the CSS files
- Changing the `global.css` `@custom-variant` declarations (those are Tailwind directives, not CSS variable definitions)

## 6. Implementation Tasks

```tasks
## Phase 1: Foundation
- [ ] T001: Create theme-loader.ts utility with loadTheme(), preloadTheme(), and ensureStoredThemeLoaded() functions | File: apps/ui/src/lib/theme-loader.ts
- [ ] T002: Replace bulk imports in theme-imports.ts with only dark.css and light.css base theme imports | File: apps/ui/src/styles/theme-imports.ts

## Phase 2: Core Integration
- [ ] T003: Update app.tsx to call ensureStoredThemeLoaded() on mount to eagerly load the user's stored theme | File: apps/ui/src/app.tsx
- [ ] T004: Update __root.tsx theme effect to call loadTheme() when deferredTheme changes | File: apps/ui/src/routes/__root.tsx

## Phase 3: Theme Picker Integration
- [ ] T005: Add preloadTheme() on hover in setup-view theme-step.tsx for instant hover preview | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/setup-view/steps/theme-step.tsx
- [ ] T006: Add preloadTheme() on hover in appearance-section.tsx theme buttons | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/settings-view/appearance/appearance-section.tsx
- [ ] T007: Add preloadTheme() on hover in project-theme-section.tsx theme buttons | File: apps/ui/src/components/views/project-settings-view/project-theme-section.tsx
```

## 7. Success Metrics

- Network tab shows only 1-2 theme CSS files loaded on initial page load (base + stored theme) instead of 40
- Initial CSS payload reduced by ~130-190KB
- Theme switching works correctly with no visible flash of unstyled content
- Hovering over themes in the setup wizard preloads them for instant preview
- Previously loaded themes are cached in memory and don't trigger re-imports
- `applyStoredTheme()` pre-hydration function continues to work (classes applied synchronously; CSS loaded eagerly)

## 8. Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Flash of unstyled content if CSS loads after class is applied | Load stored theme CSS eagerly in `app.tsx` mount; base themes (`dark`/`light`) remain synchronous imports |
| Vite dynamic import glob may not work with CSS files | Vite natively supports `import('./path.css')` for CSS - well documented pattern |
| Theme hover preview feels sluggish | Preload on mouseenter; CSS files are small (2-7KB) so load nearly instantly even on slow connections |
| CSS class applied before dynamic CSS loaded during pre-hydration | The `applyStoredTheme()` function applies the class; the CSS will be loaded very shortly after by the React mount call. Base `dark`/`light` themes are always available synchronously. For non-base themes, the brief window is negligible since global.css provides base `:root` variables. |

Update app.tsx to call ensureStoredThemeLoaded() on mount to eagerly load the user's stored theme
Update __root.tsx theme effect to call loadTheme() when deferredTheme changes
Add preloadTheme() on hover in setup-view theme-step.tsx for instant hover preview
Add preloadTheme() on hover in appearance-section.tsx theme buttons
